output: Implemented batch 5 / T5 by adding `ServerConfig.resolve_social_route()` with exact-channel, parent-channel, and guild-default fallback, introducing a scheduled publication worker on `SharingCog` that starts/stops via `cog_load`/`cog_unload`, and wiring a shared `SocialPublishService` into `main.py` so both the worker and existing caller paths use the same service instance. Verification passed with module compilation and a focused inline check that exercised route resolution directly and confirmed the worker lifecycle/main wiring from source.
deviations: Importing `SharingCog` directly under the local Python 3.8 runtime hit a pre-existing `src/common/db_handler.py` typing incompatibility (`list[int]` usage), so the worker lifecycle verification for this batch used source-level assertions after `py_compile` instead of importing the cog module in-process.

task_updates.executor_notes: Added `ServerConfig.resolve_social_route()` with exact-channel, parent-channel, and guild-default fallback behind the existing sharing feature gate. Added a scheduled publication worker to `SharingCog` using `discord.ext.tasks.loop` plus `cog_load`/`cog_unload` lifecycle hooks and wired a shared `SocialPublishService` into `main.py` so the cog worker and caller paths use the same service instance. Verified with `python -m py_compile` and a focused inline script that exercised route resolution behavior directly and checked the worker lifecycle/main wiring from source.

sense_check_acknowledgments.executor_note: Confirmed the scheduled worker starts in `cog_load` and stops in `cog_unload`, uses `discord.ext.tasks.loop` with `wait_until_ready()` gating in `before_loop`, and `main.py` now constructs and attaches one shared `SocialPublishService` instance that is passed into `SharingCog` and exposed on `bot` for sharer/admin call paths.
